explanation: The Sigma rule relies on the 'Image' field ending with '\tscon.exe'. By copying the legitimate tscon.exe binary to a different location with a different name (e.g., not_tscon.exe), the execution will no longer match the file name pattern, effectively bypassing the path-based detection while maintaining the functionality needed for RDP session hijacking.

The rule is specifically scoped to the binary 'tscon.exe'. An attacker can use alternative methods or custom-compiled tools that interact with the Remote Desktop Services API (like WTSConnectSession) directly. Since these tools do not use the tscon.exe image, they do not trigger the specific file-name selection criteria of this rule.
